title: Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris
summary: A SpaceX Starlink satellite experienced an on-orbit malfunction, generating debris. This is the second such incident in just over three months.

title: Rocket Lab wins German approval for Mynaric deal
summary: Rocket Lab has received German regulatory approval for its acquisition of Mynaric, paving the way for the deal to close in April after months of scrutiny. The decision addresses concerns over maintaining key space technology within domestic control.

title: Varda flies navigation payload, heat shield tests on sixth reentry mission
summary: Varda Space conducted its sixth reentry mission, testing navigation payloads and heat shield technology during a high-speed descent. The mission included hardware from Rhea Space, Sandia, and NASA to gather critical data.

title: ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) launched the first two Celeste satellites aboard a Rocket Lab Electron launcher to test a complementary LEO navigation layer. The mission aims to demonstrate a new navigation system in a quasi-polar low Earth orbit.

title: SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission
summary: SpaceX launched the Transporter-16 rideshare mission on March 30, deploying over 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

title: China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit
summary: China's Kinetica-2 rocket successfully completed its maiden flight, carrying a prototype cargo spacecraft into orbit. The launch was conducted by CAS Space, marking a significant milestone for the Chinese space industry.

title: Pentagon weighing termination of Raytheon GPS ground control contract after years of delays
summary: The Pentagon is considering terminating Raytheon's GPS ground control contract due to years of delays. The U.S. Space Force is exploring alternatives, including shifting to an upgraded legacy ground system.

title: The race to build orbital data centers is missing its biggest variable: power
summary: The space industry is racing to build orbital data centers, but the critical challenge of power supply remains unaddressed. Major players like Elon Musk, LoneStar, and NVIDIA have made headlines with their plans, yet the source of electricity for these facilities is unclear.

title: With Voyager’s help, Icarus Robotics to test free-flyer on ISS
summary: Voyager Technologies will assist Icarus Robotics in testing a free-flying platform, Joyride, on the International Space Station. The collaboration includes payload integration, safety certification, and launch coordination for a 2027 mission.

title: Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million in funding, achieving a $1.1 billion valuation. The company plans to deploy an 88,000-strong orbital data center network pending regulatory approval.

title: Huge solar flare no threat to Artemis 2 astronaut launch to the moon, NASA says
summary: A massive X1.4 solar flare is not expected to threaten NASA's Artemis 2 mission, which aims to send four astronauts to the moon.

title: Record-breaking 'space laser' erupts from merging galaxies 8 billion light-years away
summary: Astronomers discovered the most distant and brightest 'space laser' from merging galaxies 8 billion light-years away, occurring when the universe was half its current age.

title: 25 years ago, 'Invader Zim' arrived, but it was too far ahead of its time
summary: The animated series 'Invader Zim' premiered 25 years ago, noted for its futuristic themes that were ahead of its time. The show's dark humor and unique style have since gained a cult following.

title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS may be nearly 12 billion years old — so ancient its star system may no longer exist
summary: The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is estimated to be nearly 12 billion years old, potentially making it as ancient as the Milky Way itself. Its star system may no longer exist due to its extreme age.

title: Darth Maul was originally going to be the villain of the 'Star Wars' sequels; here's why that would have been a bad idea
summary: George Lucas initially considered making Darth Maul the primary antagonist for the 'Star Wars' sequel trilogy, but this idea was ultimately abandoned due to various reasons.

title: Heading to Florida for NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch? Here's what to know before you go
summary: Florida is expecting a large influx of visitors for NASA's historic Artemis 2 moon launch, requiring extra time for attendees to see the astronauts. The event is a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: Best moon movies: 12 lunar adventures to watch ahead of NASA's Artemis 2 launch
summary: An article lists 12 lunar-themed movies to watch in anticipation of NASA's upcoming Artemis 2 mission. The films span from classic to modern depictions of moon adventures.

title: Moon menu: Here's what the Artemis 2 astronauts will eat during their historic mission (video)
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts will have specially designed meals for their 10-day lunar mission, showcasing innovative space food solutions. NASA is highlighting the unique challenges of dining in a confined spacecraft during the historic journey.

title: Sun rises on Artemis 2 launch pad | Space photo of the day for March 30, 2026
summary: NASA's SLS moon rocket for the Artemis 2 mission is seen silhouetted against the rising sun at the launch pad. The launch is scheduled for April 1, 2026.

title: 'The sasquatch is honesty': Inside Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen's Artemis 2 mission patch
summary: Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen incorporated Indigenous perspectives into his personal mission patch for NASA's Artemis 2 mission. This marks a unique blend of space exploration and cultural representation.

title: Fires Tear Through Nebraska Grasslands
summary: Extreme fire activity tore through Nebraska grasslands in March 2026 due to dry, warm, and windy conditions across the U.S. Great Plains.

title: Sendoff for Artemis II Crew
summary: The Artemis II crew, including NASA and CSA astronauts, posed for a photo before a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas and Gibbons are designated as backup crew members for the mission.

title: Seeing Blue During Schirmacher’s Summer Melt Season
summary: A network of meltwater lakes and drainage channels turned an Antarctic ice shelf known for its blue ice areas even bluer during the summer melt season. This phenomenon highlights the impact of seasonal changes on glacial landscapes.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon. His career has focused on enabling spacecraft communication with Earth.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA has selected Intuitive Machines for a $180.4 million contract to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission includes seven payloads, five of which are NASA-funded, to enhance lunar research.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the categories for its Environment and Energy 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth observation satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier in Washington on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface imagery of the Pacific Northwest.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's technology allows it to observe the surface despite cloudy conditions in the Pacific Northwest.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The demonstrations aim to test thermal protection systems, advance communications, and deepen Earth's atmosphere understanding.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has chosen 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including instrument deployment and sample collection. These scientists will help guide astronauts in conducting critical observations and experiments on the Moon.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights advancements in space transportation for deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit aboard the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another significant deployment of small satellites for various customers.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028, with Administrator Jared Isaacman and agency leaders detailing the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on Starbase launch pads to support the upcoming Block 3 Starships, highlighting their focus on increasing launch frequency. The ground activity reflects the company's ongoing efforts to enhance infrastructure for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the ISS from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired Site 31/6, following a previous Soyuz MS-28 launch from the same pad. The mission marks a return to operations after pad repairs.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the manufacturing of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket while also revealing plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website provides industry news related to the space sector, though no specific details are provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions in space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly-induced blackout. The mission team confirmed the successful recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events for industry to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events will outline planned activities, thematic priorities, and industrial opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA is introducing ECOS version 6, a new costing software, which will be mandatory for its suppliers starting March 30, 2026. The software will be used for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture. This innovation aims to replace bulky and expensive antennas currently used.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic systems. This initiative aims to advance space technology research and education.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: Transporter-16 successfully launched 119 payloads into orbit. This marks the latest in a series of rideshare missions to space.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud secured $170 million in a Series A funding round, valuing the company at $1.1 billion. The investment underscores investor interest in orbital data center ventures and Starcloud's proven flight heritage.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million for its in-space propulsion technology. The company aims to scale production after flight-qualifying its core technologies.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology, which offers stronger, more accurate, and more secure PNT signals in low Earth orbit compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, marked 100 days in office with a bold announcement about returning to the Moon. The statement emphasizes an accelerated push for lunar missions.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them in a difficult position. This highlights Europe's significant but potentially stranded contributions to the Artemis program.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's revised vision for Commercial Low-Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has raised concerns and confusion among policymakers regarding its direction and feasibility.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: The article discusses directed energy as an alternative to traditional space-based interceptors (SBIs). It highlights this technology as a potential solution in the space defense sector.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Commerce Department introduced a one-stop licensing proposal for novel space missions, aiming to streamline regulatory authority amid ongoing debates over mission authorization frameworks.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M to Fund Orbital Data Center Plans
summary: Starcloud, a space startup, raised $170 million in a Series A funding round to develop orbital data centers using satellites. The company is now valued at $1.1 billion.

title: WAVE Achieves First Cloud-to-Gateway Satcom Virtualization with AI Signal Analysis
summary: WAVE Consortium, an IEEE standards body, achieved the first standardized waveform virtualization using FPGA acceleration in a cloud-to-gateway satcom system with AI signal analysis.

title: SpaceX Launches 119 Payloads on the Transporter-16 Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads into Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) on the Transporter-16 rideshare mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base. This mission marks another milestone in SpaceX's dedicated smallsat rideshare program.

title: Live in the Booth: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski Talks Spectrum Strategy and Defense Potential
summary: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski discusses the company's spectrum strategy and defense potential during SATShow Week, highlighting the recent launch of the BlueBird 6 satellite.

title: SpaceBridge Launches UniHub as Streamlined VSAT Platform
summary: SpaceBridge launched its new UniHub, a compact all-in-one VSAT hub platform designed to simplify satellite network deployment while offering high performance, efficiency, and scalability.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler outlined Northwood Space's vision for rapidly deployable satellite ground infrastructure, aiming to solve the challenges of ground system deployment. The company is developing an end-to-end ground system to address these issues.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The conversation highlights advancements in these systems from the AvL booth.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market's outlook due to volatility. Discussions focused on direct-to-direct (D2D) services, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discussed the company's RF solutions, including phased arrays for LEO satellites, during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Qorvo's advancements in satellite communication technology.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are increasingly prioritizing sovereign control over their satellite communications networks due to geopolitical uncertainties. The challenge lies in defining what sovereign space means and its capabilities within a more complex geopolitical space infrastructure.
